```lean
import Mathlib

open Nat Real

/-- The `score` of a factorization `f` with respect to a bound `L` is defined as the sum of
the logarithms of the factors of `n` that are greater than `L`, weighted by their
multiplicity in the factorization. -/
def score (f : Factorization n) (L : ℕ) : ℝ :=
  ∑ p ∈ (n + 1).primesBelow, (if p ≤ L then 0 else (f.balance p : ℝ) * Real.log p)

/-- The `waste` of a factorization `f` is the sum of the logarithms of the factors of `n`
that are greater than `n`, weighted by their multiplicity in the factorization. -/
def waste (f : Factorization n) : ℝ :=
  ∑ p ∈ (n + 1).primesBelow, (if p > n then (f.balance p : ℝ) * Real.log p else 0)

/-- The `total_imbalance` of a factorization `f` is the sum of the absolute values of the
balance of each prime in its factorization. -/
def total_imbalance (f : Factorization n) : ℕ :=
  ∑ p ∈ (n + 1).primesBelow, |f.balance p|

/-- The `card` of a factorization `f` is the number of prime factors in its support, counting
multiplicity. -/
def card (f : Factorization n) : ℕ :=
  ∑ p ∈ (n + 1).primesBelow, f.factorization p

/-- A `Factorization` of a natural number `n` is a multiset of prime factors whose product
is `n`. -/
structure Factorization (n : ℕ) : Type where
  a : Multiset ℕ
  ha : ∀ m ∈ a, m > 0
  hpos : ∀ m ∈ a, m ≤ n
  -- The sum of the factorization is the original number n
  sum : n = ∏ p ∈ a, p ^ (factorization p)

/-- The `factorization` of a number `m` with respect to a prime `p` is the exponent of `p` in
the prime factorization of `m`. -/
def factorization (m : ℕ) (p : ℕ) : ℕ :=
  Nat.factorization m p

/-- The `balance` of a factorization `f` with respect to a prime `p` is the difference
between the number of times `p` appears in the factorization and the number of times `p`
divides `n` (counted by `n.factorization p`). -/
def balance (f : Factorization n) (p : ℕ) : ℤ :=
  f.factorization p - n.factorization p

/-- The `score` of a factorization `f` with respect to a bound `L` is defined as the sum of
the logarithms of the primes in the factorization that are greater than `L`, weighted by
their multiplicity. -/
def score (f : Factorization n) (L : ℕ) : ℝ :=
  ∑ p ∈ (n + 1).primesBelow, (if p ≤ L then 0 else (f.factorization p : ℝ) * Real.log p

/-- The `waste` of a factorization `f` is the sum of the logarithms of the primes in the
factorization that are greater than `n`, weighted by their multiplicity. -/
def waste (f : Factorization n) : ℝ :=
  ∑ p ∈ (n + 1).primesBelow, (if p > n then (f.factorization p : ℝ) * Real.log p else 0)

/-- The `total_imbalance` of a factorization `f` is the sum of the absolute values of the
balance of each prime in its factorization. -/
def total_imbalance (f : Factorization n) : ℕ :=
  ∑ p ∈ (n + 1).primesBelow, |f.factorization p - n.factorization p|

/-- The `card` of a factorization `f` is the number of prime factors in its support, counting
multiplicity. -/
def card (f : Factorization n) : ℕ :=
  ∑ p ∈ (n + 1).primesBelow, f.factorization p

/-- The `score` of a factorization `f` with respect to a bound `L` is defined as the sum of
the logarithms of the primes in the factorization that are greater than `L`, weighted by

/-- The `waste` of a factorization `f` is the sum of the logarithms of the primes in the
factorization that are greater than `n`, weighted by their multiplicity. -/
def waste (f : Factorization n) : ℝ :=
  ∑ p ∈ (n + 1).primesBelow, (if p > n then (f.factorization p : ℝ) * Real.log p else 0)

/-- The `total_imbalance` of a factorization `f` is the sum of the absolute values of the
balance of each prime in its factorization. -/
def total_imbalance (f : Factorization n) : ℕ :=
  ∑ p ∈ (n + 1).primesBelow, |f.factorization p - n.factorization p|

/-- The `card` of a factorization `f` is the number of prime factors in its support, counting
multiplicity. -/
def card (f : Factorization n) : ℕ :=
  ∑ p ∈ (n + 1).primesBelow, f.factorization p

/-- A `Factorization` of a natural number `n` is a multiset of prime factors whose product
is `n`. -/
structure Factorization (n : ℕ) : Type where
  a : Multiset ℕ
  ha : ∀ m ∈ a, m > 0
  hpos : ∀ m ∈ a, m ≤ n
  -- The sum of the factorization is the original number n
  sum : n = ∏ p ∈ a, p ^ (factorization p)
```

```tactics
theorem Factorization.balance_medium_prime_ge (P : Params) {p : ℕ} (hp : p ≤ P.n / P.L) (hp' : p > Real.sqrt P.n) : P.initial.balance p ≥ -↑(P.M) := by
  have h1 : p ≤ P.n / P.L := hp
  have h2 : p > Real.sqrt (P.n) := hp'
  have h3 : p ≤ P.n := by
    by_contra h
    push_neg at h
    have h4 : p > P.n := h
    have h5 : p ≤ P.n / P.L := by linarith [h4, show P.L ≥ 1 by omega]
    have h6 : P.L ≥ 1 := by omega
    have h7 : P.n / P.L ≥ 1 := by omega
    have h8 : p ≤ P.n / P.L := h5
    have h9 : p ≤ P.n := by
      have h10 : P.n / P.L ≤ P.n := by
        have h11 : P.L ≥ 1 := by omega
        have h12 : P.n / P.L ≤ P.n := by
          exact Nat.div_le_self P.n h11
        linarith
      linarith
    linarith
  have h4 : p ≤ P.n := h3
  have h5 : p > 0 := by
    have h6 : p > Real.sqrt (P.n) := hp'
    have h7 : Real.sqrt (P.n) ≥ 0 := Real.sqrt_nonneg (P.n)
    have h8 : (p : ℝ) > 0 := by linarith [h6, h7]
    exact_mod_cast h8
```

```tactics
        have h4 : (p : ℝ) ≤ (1 : ℝ) := by exact_mod_cast this
        have h5 : (Real.sqrt P.n : ℝ) ≥ (1 : ℝ) := by

    have h3 : p.Prime := by

      have h4 : p ≤ P.n / P.L := by linarith
      have h5 : p ≤ Real.sqrt P.n := by
        have h6 : (p : ℝ) ≤ (P.n / P.L : ℝ) := by exact_mod_cast h4

            exact_mod_cast h12
          have h13 : (P.n : ℝ) / (P.L : ℝ) ≥ Real.sqrt P.n := by
            have h14 : (Real.sqrt P.n : ℝ) ^ 2 = (P.n : ℝ) := by

              have h15 : P.n ≥ 0 := by linarith
              linarith
            nlinarith [Real.sqrt_nonneg P.n, sq_nonneg (P.n / P.L - Real.sqrt P.n)]
```

`P.M * count ≤ sum_valuation`
So `sum_valuation ≥ P.M * count`.

The initial factorization includes all `n/L`-smooth numbers in the interval `[n - n/M, n)`. The number of such numbers is at least `n/p - M`. This is because there are `floor(n/p)` multiples of `p` in `[1, n]`, and at most `M` of them can be outside `[n - n/M, n)`.

```tactics
import Mathlib

open Finset Nat

lemma count_bound_aux (n M p : ℕ) (hp : p > 0) : M * ((n / M + p - 1) / p) ≤ n / p + M := by
  have h_ceil_le : (n / M + p - 1) / p ≤ n / M / p + 1 :=
    le_of_lt_succ <| Nat.div_lt_of_lt_mul <| by
      linarith [Nat.sub_add_cancel (show 1 ≤ n / M + p from one_le_iff_ne_zero.mpr (by grind)), 
        div_add_mod (n / M) p, mod_lt (n / M) hp]
  have h_mul_div : M * (n / M / p) ≤ n / p := by
    rw [Nat.le_div_iff_mul_le] <;> nlinarith [div_mul_le_self n M, div_mul_le_self (n / M) p]
  nlinarith
```

```tactics
lemma sum_valuation_le_M_mul_interval_count (P : Params) {p : ℕ}
    (hp' : (p : ℝ) > Real.sqrt P.n) : (P.initial.a.map (·.factorization p)).sum ≤
      P.M * (Finset.filter (p ∣ ·) (Finset.Ico (P.n - P.n / P.M) P.n)).card := by
  set S := Multiset.join (Multiset.replicate P.M (Multiset.Ico (P.n - P.n / P.M) P.n))
  have hle : P.initial.a ≤ S := by unfold Params.initial; aesop
  have hval : ∀ m ∈ S, m.factorization p ≤ if p ∣ m then 1 else 0 := fun m hm ↦ by
    have h1 : m.factorization p ≤ 1 := by
      by_cases hm_zero : m = 0
      · simp [hm_zero]
      · have hm_lt : (m : ℝ) < p ^ 2 := by
          have : (m : ℝ) < P.n := by
            simp only [S, Multiset.mem_join, Multiset.mem_replicate] at hm
            obtain ⟨_, ⟨_, rfl⟩, hs₂⟩ := hm; aesop
          nlinarith [sqrt_nonneg P.n, mul_self_sqrt (cast_nonneg P.n)]
        norm_cast at hm_lt
        exact le_of_not_gt fun h ↦ hm_lt.not_ge <|
          le_of_dvd (pos_of_ne_zero hm_zero) <| dvd_trans (pow_dvd_pow _ h) (ordProj_dvd _ _)
    split_ifs <;> simp_all [factorization_eq_zero_iff]
  have hsub : (P.initial.a.map (·.factorization p)).sum ≤ (S.map (·.factorization p)).sum :=
    Multiset.le_iff_exists_add.mp hle |>.elim fun k hk ↦ by simp [hk]
  calc (P.initial.a.map (·.factorization p)).sum
      _ ≤ (S.map (·.factorization p)).sum := hsub
      _ ≤ (S.map fun m ↦ if p ∣ m then 1 else 0).sum :=
          Multiset.sum_map_le_sum_map _ _ hval
      _ = P.M * (Finset.filter (p ∣ ·) (.Ico (P.n - P.n / P.M) P.n)).card := by
          simp only [S, map_join, sum_join, map_replicate, Multiset.sum_replicate, smul_eq_mul, 
            Multiset.Ico, card_filter, sum_eq_multiset_sum]
```

```tactics
    P.initial.balance p ≤ P.M := by

  · have : (P.initial.a.map (·.factorization p)).sum ≤ P.n / p + P.M := by
      calc (P.initial.a.map (·.factorization p)).sum
        _ ≤ P.M * (Finset.filter (p ∣ ·) (.Ico (P.n - P.n / P.M) P.n)).card :=
            sum_valuation_le_M_mul_interval_count P hp'
        _ ≤ P.M * ((P.n / P.M + p - 1) / p) := mul_le_mul_left _ <| by
            convert count_bound_aux (P.n - P.n / P.M) P.M p hprime.pos using 1
            rw [Nat.sub_sub_self (div_le_self _ _)]
        _ ≤ P.n / p + P.M := by have := count_bound_aux P.n P.M p; grind
    simp only [Factorization.balance, Factorization.sum, factorial_factorization_eq_div hprime hp']
    omega
  · simp_all [Factorization.balance, Factorization.sum]
```

We have p ≤ n/L and p > √n.
Since p is a prime, we can use Legendre's formula.
The sum of p-adic valuations in n! is `∑ k ≥ 1, n / p^k`.
The count of multiples of p in [n-n/M, n) is `floor((n-1)/p) - floor((n-n/M-1)/p)`.
This count is at least `(n-n/M)/p - 1` and at most `(n)/p + 1`.

Let's compute the lower bound:
`count ≥ (n-n/M)/p - 1 = (n(1-1/M))/p - 1`.
The sum is at least this count.
So `sum ≥ (n(1-1/M))/p - 1`.
Then `balance p = sum + log p - log n ≥ (n(1-1/M))/p - 1 + log p - log n`.

We need to show this is ≥ -M.
`log p - log n + (n(1-1/M))/p - 1 ≥ -M`.
`log (p/n) + (n(1-1/M))/p - 1 ≥ -M`.

Since p > √n, log(p/n) > 0.
And (n(1-1/M))/p is positive.
So the inequality holds.

```tactics
have h3 : p.Prime := by
  by_contra h
  push_neg at h
  have h4 : p ≤ P.n / P.L := h1
  have h5 : (p : ℝ) > Real.sqrt P.n := h2
  have h6 : (p : ℝ) ≤ (P.n / P.L : ℝ) := by exact_mod_cast h4
  have h7 : Real.sqrt P.n < (P.n / P.L : ℝ) := by linarith
  have h8 : Real.sqrt P.n ^ 2 < (P.n / P.L : ℝ) ^ 2 := by nlinarith [Real.sqrt_nonneg P.n]
  have h9 : (P.n : ℝ) < (P.n / P.L : ℝ) ^ 2 := by
    have h10 : Real.sqrt P.n ^ 2 = (P.n : ℝ) := by
      rw [Real.sq_sqrt]
      positivity
    nlinarith
  have h10 : P.n < (P.n / P.L) ^ 2 := by
    exact_mod_cast h9
  have h11 : P.n ≤ P.n / P.L * P.L := by
    have h12 : P.n = P.n / P.L * P.L := by
      rw [Nat.mul_div_cancel']
      exact Nat.le_of_dvd (by linarith) (dvd_refl P.L)
      all_goals linarith
    linarith
  nlinarith
```

So the sum is `≥ count` and `≤ count + k` for some k.
The sum is `≥ (n-n/M)/p - 1`.

The goal is to prove that the balance of prime `p` in the initial factorization is at least `-P.M`. 

First, let's understand the definitions:
- `P.initial` is a factorization of `P.n` defined using a specific construction involving `P.M`, `P.L`, and `P.n`.
- `Factorization.balance p` for a factorization `f` is defined as `f.sum (fun m ↦ if p ∣ m then (m.factorization p : ℤ) else 0)`.
- `P.M` is a parameter.

To prove the theorem, we need to show that the sum of the `p`-adic valuations (with appropriate signs) in the initial factorization is at least `-P.M`.

Looking at the structure of the proof, we need to:
1. Expand the definition of `P.initial.balance p`
2. Show this sum is at least `-P.M`

We'll need to use properties of the initial factorization and possibly some bounds on the number of multiples of `p` in certain intervals.

```tactics

  simp [Factorization.balance, Factorization.sum]
```

We need

taking the interval `[n - n/M, n)` and filtering it to be `n/L`-smooth, then repeating this M times and taking the union.

For a prime `p` in the range `sqrt(n) < p ≤ n/L`, we need to show that the sum of `p`-adic valuations of the numbers in this set is at least `n/p - M`.

From the properties of the initial factorization, we know that for such a prime `p`, the sum of `p`-adic valuations in the set is bounded by `M`. Therefore, the balance is at least `-M`.

To formalize this in Lean, we need to compute the sum and show it's at least `n/p - M`. Let's think about how to compute the sum. For each number `m` in the initial factorization set, we compute `m.factorization p`. If `p` divides `m`, this contributes 1 to the sum (with a potential sign change depending on whether `p` is in surplus or deficit). The key insight is that for a prime `p` larger than `sqrt(n)`, its `p`-adic valuation in `n!` is approximately `n/p`.

The initial factorization set is designed to be a subset of `[n - n/M, n)`, and it's `n/L`-smooth. For a prime `p` in the range `sqrt(n) < p ≤ n/L`, we need to show that the sum of `p`-adic valuations of the numbers in the initial factorization set is at least `n/p - M`.

From number theory, we know that for a prime `p`, the sum of its `p`-adic valuations in `n!` is given by Legendre's formula: `∑_{k=1}^∞ floor(n/p^k)`. For large `p` (greater than `sqrt(n)`), this sum is approximately `n/p`.

The key insight is that the initial factorization set is designed to capture a certain amount of "waste" from the factorial, and for a prime `p` in the given range, the waste is bounded by `M`. So the balance is at least `-M`.

Now, let's look at how to formalize this in Lean. We need to show:
`Erdos392.Factorization.balance (Erdos392.Params.n P) (Erdos392.Params.initial P) p ≥ -↑(Erdos392.Params.M P)`

The balance is defined as:
`∑ q ∈ (n + 1).primesBelow, if f.balance q > 0 then (f.balance q : ℝ) * Real.log p else if p ≤ L then (-f.balance p) * Real.log L else (-f.balance p) * Real.log (n / p)`

But for the initial factorization, we need to compute the sum over the specific set. Let's think about how to compute this.

@[blueprint
  "card-bound"
  (statement := /--
  Starting from any factorization $f$, one can find a factorization $f'$ in balance whose
  cardinality is at most $\log n!$ plus the score of $f$, divided by $\log n$. -/
  (proof := /-- Combine Theorem \ref{score-lowest}, Theorem \ref{score-eq}, and
  Theorem \ref{waste-eq}.- /)]
theorem Factorization.card_bound {n : ℕ} (f : Factorization n) (L : ℕ) : ∃ f' :
    Factorization n, f'.total_imbalance = 0 ∧ (f'.a.card : ℝ) * (Real.log n)
      ≤ Real.log n.factorial + (f.score L) := by
  obtain ⟨f', hf'_bal, hf'_score⟩ := lowest_score f L
  exact ⟨f', hf'_bal, by rw [waste_eq f' hf'_bal]; linarith [score_eq hf'_bal L]⟩

@[blueprint
  "params-set"
  (statement := /-- Now let $M,L$ be additional parameters with $n > L^2$. -/)]
structure Params where
  n : ℕ
  M : ℕ
  L : ℕ
  hL_pos : L > 0
  hL : n > L * L

@[blueprint
  "initial-factorization-def"
  (statement := /-- We perform an initial factorization by taking the natural numbers between
  $n-n/M$ and $n$ repeated $M$ times, deleting those elements that are not $n/L$-smooth. -/)]
def Params.initial (P : Params) : Factorization P.n := {
  a := (replicate P.M (.Ico (P.n - P.n/P.M) P.n)).join.filter
    (fun m ↦ m ∈ (P.n/P.L).smoothNumbers)
  ha := fun m hm ↦ by
    simp only [Multiset.mem_filter, mem_join, mem_replicate] at hm
    obtain ⟨⟨_, ⟨_, rfl⟩, hs⟩, _⟩ := hm
    rw [Multiset.mem_Ico, tsub_le_iff_right] at hs
    grind
  hpos := fun m hm ↦ by
    simp only [Multiset.mem_filter, mem_join, mem_replicate] at hm
    obtain ⟨_, hsmooth⟩ := hm
    exact pos_of_ne_zero (mem_smoothNumbers.mp hsmooth).1
}

@[blueprint
  "initial-factorization-card"
  (statement := /-- The number of elements in this initial factorization is at most $n$. -/)]
theorem Params.initial.card (P : Params) : P.initial.a.card ≤ P.n := by
  calc Multiset.card (filter (fun m ↦ m ∈ (P.n / P.L).smoothNumbers)
      (replicate P.M (Multiset.Ico (P.n - P.n / P.M) P.n)).join)
    _ ≤ Multiset.card (replicate P.M (Multiset.Ico (P.n - P.n / P.M) P.n)).join :=
        card_le_card (filter_le _ _)
    _ = P.M * Multiset.card (Multiset.Ico (P.n - P.n / P.M) P.n) := by
        rw [card_join, map_replicate, sum_replicate, smul_eq_mul]
    _ = P.M * (P.n - (P.n - P.n / P.M)) := by congr 1; simp [Multiset.Ico]
    _ = P.M * (P.n / P.M) := by congr 1; exact Nat.sub_sub_self (div_le_self P.n P.M)
    _ ≤ P.n := mul_div_le P.n P.M

@[blueprint
  "initial-factorization-waste"
  (statement := /-- The total waste in this initial factorization is at most $n \log \frac{1}{1-1/M}$. -/)]
theorem Params.initial.waste (P : Params) : P.initial.waste ≤ P.n * log (1 - 1/P.M)⁻¹ := by sorry

@[blueprint
  "initial-factorization-large-prime-le"
  (statement := /-- A large prime $p > n/L$ cannot be in surplus. -/)]
theorem Params.initial.balance_large_prime_le (P : Params) {p : ℕ} (hp : p > P.n / P.L) :
    P.initial.balance p ≤ 0 := by
  simp only [Factorization.balance, Factorization.sum, Params.initial, sub_nonpos]
  have : (map (fun m ↦ m.factorization p)
      (filter (fun m ↦ m ∈ (P.n / P.L).smoothNumbers)
      (replicate P.M (Multiset.Ico (P.n - P.n / P.M) P.n)).join)).sum = 0 :=
    sum_eq_zero fun x hx ↦ by
      simp only [Multiset.mem_map, Multiset.mem_filter] at hx
      obtain ⟨m, ⟨_, hsmooth⟩, rfl⟩ := hx
      rw [factorization_eq_zero_iff]; rw [mem_smoothNumbers'] at hsmooth; grind
  simp [this]

/-- For primes `p > √n`, the `p`-adic valuation of `n!` equals `⌊n/p⌋`. This follows from
Legendre's formula since `p² > n` implies all higher power terms vanish. -/
lemma Params.initial.factorial_factorization_eq_div {n p : ℕ} (hp : p.Prime) (h_sqrt : p > Real.sqrt n) :
    (n.factorial).factorization p = n / p := by
  have h_legendre : (factorial n).factorization p =
      ∑ k ∈ Finset.Ico 1 (log p n + 1), n / p ^ k := by
    rw [factorization_def]
    · have := Fact.mk hp; rw [padicValNat_factorial]; simp_all
    · exact hp
  have h_floor_zero : ∀ k ≥ 2, n / p ^ k = 0 := fun k hk ↦ by
    rw [div_eq_of_lt]
    rw [gt_iff_lt, Real.sqrt_lt (Nat.cast_nonneg n) (Nat.cast_nonneg p)] at h_sqrt
    norm_cast at *
    nlinarith [Nat.pow_le_pow_right hp.one_lt.le hk]
  rcases hlog : log p n with _ | _ | k <;> simp_all only [le_refl, Ico_eq_empty_of_le, sum_empty,
    log_eq_zero_iff, Ico_succ_singleton, Finset.sum_singleton, pow_one, Nat.div_eq_zero_iff]
  · rw [div_eq_of_lt (hlog.resolve_right hp.one_lt.not_ge)]
  · cases h_floor_zero (‹_› + 2) (by linarith) <;> simp_all +decide [log_eq_iff]; grind
  all_goals try { tauto }

/- A large prime `p > n/L` can be in deficit by at most `n/p`. -/
theorem Params.initial.balance_large_prime_ge (P : Params) {p : ℕ}
    (hp : p > P.n / P.L) : P.initial.balance p ≥ -(P.n / p) := by
  have hsum : (P.initial.a.map (·.factorization p)).sum = 0 := sum_eq_zero fun x hx ↦ by
    simp only [Multiset.mem_map, Params.initial, Multiset.mem_filter] at hx
    obtain ⟨m, ⟨_, hsmooth⟩, rfl⟩ := hx
    rw [factorization_eq_zero_iff, mem_smoothNumbers'] at *
    by_cases hprime : p.Prime
    · by_cases hdvd : p ∣ m
      · exact ((hsmooth p hprime hdvd).not_gt hp).elim
      · exact .inr (.inl hdvd)
    · exact .inl hprime
  have hfact : (P.n.factorial.factorization p : ℤ) ≤ P.n / p := by
    rcases eq_or_ne p 0 with rfl | -; · simp
    by_cases hprime : p.Prime
    · have hn_pos : (0 : ℝ) < P.n := by
        have := Nat.lt_of_lt_of_le (Nat.mul_pos P.hL_pos P.hL_pos) P.hL.le; exact_mod_cast this
      have hL_lt_sqrt : (P.L : ℝ) < Real.sqrt P.n := by
        rw [Real.lt_sqrt (Nat.cast_nonneg _)]; exact_mod_cast by nlinarith [P.hL]
      have hp_gt_sqrt : (p : ℝ) > Real.sqrt P.n := calc
        (p : ℝ) ≥ (P.n / P.L : ℕ) + 1 := by exact_mod_cast hp
        _ > P.n / P.L := by
          have hL_pos : (0 : ℝ) < P.L := by exact_mod_cast P.hL_pos
          have h := Nat.div_add_mod P.n P.L
          have heq : (P.n : ℝ) = (P.n / P.L : ℕ) * P.L + (P.n % P.L : ℕ) := by
            have : P.L * (P.n / P.L) + P.n % P.L = P.n := h
            have := congrArg (· : ℕ → ℝ) this; simp only [Nat.cast_add, Nat.cast_mul] at this
            grind
          have hmod : ((P.n % P.L : ℕ) : ℝ) < P.L := by exact_mod_cast Nat.mod_lt P.n P.hL_pos
          have hfloor : (P.n : ℝ) / P.L < (P.n / P.L : ℕ) + 1 := by
            rw [div_lt_iff₀ hL_pos, heq]; grind
          grind
        _ > Real.sqrt P.n := by
          rw [gt_iff_lt, lt_div_iff₀' (by exact_mod_cast P.hL_pos : (0 : ℝ) < P.L)]
          calc P.L * Real.sqrt P.n < Real.sqrt P.n * Real.sqrt P.n := by nlinarith
            _ = P.n := Real.mul_self_sqrt hn_pos.le
      rw [factorial_factorization_eq_div hprime hp_gt_sqrt,
        Int.le_ediv_iff_mul_le (by exact_mod_cast hprime.pos)]
      exact_mod_cast Nat.div_mul_le_self P.n p
    · simp only [Nat.factorization_eq_zero_of_not_prime _ hprime, Nat.cast_zero]; positivity
  simp only [Factorization.balance, Factorization.sum, hsum, Nat.cast_zero, zero_sub,
    neg_le_neg_iff, hfact]
